subject: Re: tensorflow error when particle picking
body:
Hi Olivier,
I met the same problem as you.  I tried to pick apoferritin particles but 
it didn't pick correctly at all. Did you fix this problem? How did you fix 
it? Thank you so much.

Best,
Xiong

在2022年1月10日星期一 UTC-5 04:14:26<olivier....@outlook.com> 写道：

> So I was wrong, Warp lets you select a model even though the model file is 
> not in the right path, all it looks at is the model folder apparently. 
> After placing the model file in the right path (the model file was located 
> in a sub-directory initially) I got rid of the error message. Now I get the 
> same problem as the other users; the model loads but it takes about 20 min 
> for the model to load.
>
> I tried to pick apoferritin particles but it picks garbage so i am 
> wondering why. I tried the non-mask model. Are there specific settings that 
> need to be set in order for the model to pick correctly? The only setting I 
> see is the expected particle size, does it need to be set to a specific 
> size for things to work? I wanted to check for documentation on the WARP 
> website but it seems to be offline.
>
> Best,
>
> Olivier
>
> On Wednesday, 5 January 2022 at 16:45:39 UTC+1 Olivier Le Bihan wrote:
>
>> The picking model is in the right path. A model can only be selected in 
>> the Warp GUI if it is in the correct directory (you can't choose the 
>> directory where it searches for picking models).
>>
>> The fact Warp knows the name of the picking model (in the error message) 
>> prooves that it is in the correct directory.
>>
>> Maybe a permissions problem since I use a session that doesn't have admin 
>> privileges, but I took care to install Warp in a directory that is 
>> accessible without admin privileges (I saw that advice somewhere in the 
>> documentation). But I'll check on that and let you know. Thanks for your 
>> inputs.
>>
>> Best,
>>
>> Olivier
>>
>> On Wednesday, 5 January 2022 at 13:40:03 UTC+1 Pranav Shah wrote:
>>
>>> As error messages go, it is quite self-explanatory. Have you tried 
>>> storing the picking model in the path specified in the error message? 
>>> Best, 
>>> Pranav 
>>> -- 
>>> Pranav Shah 
>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow. 
>>>
>>> Division of Structural Biology, 
>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics, 
>>> University of Oxford, 
>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN, 
>>> UK 
>>>
>>> On Wed, Jan 5, 2022 at 12:28 PM Olivier Le Bihan 
>>> <olivier....@outlook.com> wrote: 
>>> > 
>>> > Hi Pranav, 
>>> > 
>>> > Thank you for your feedback. Yes I have seen that post but users 
>>> mainly complain about very slow particles picking, my problem is more 
>>> severe since I can't pick at all. 
>>> > 
>>> > The error message I got is the exact same as Dominique's error (see 
>>> Dominique's screenshot of error message in the first message of this 
>>> thread). The error message is pointing to a problem with TensorFlow but it 
>>> could be a silly mistake from my side since it indicates a problem reading 
>>> the model. 
>>> > 
>>> > As more and more users will switch to recent GPU hardware, this 
>>> problem is going to be a major limitation for Warp's usability by the 
>>> community I suppose. The RTX 3090 launched in september 2020, it is not 
>>> particularly new anymore and my hope is that Warp will ship with TensorFlow 
>>> 2.0 in the near future. That being said, I really like Warp otherwise and I 
>>> can totally understand the amount of work that would be needed to adapt the 
>>> code for TF 2.0... 
>>> > 
>>> > So I am wondering if the source of my problem (picking not working at 
>>> all and rising an error message) is the same as others problem (slow 
>>> picking) or are these problems unrelated. 
>>> > 
>>> > Best, 
>>> > 
>>> > Olivier 
>>> > 
>>> > 
>>> > 
>>> > On Wednesday, 5 January 2022 at 11:01:01 UTC+1 Pranav Shah wrote: 
>>> >> 
>>> >> Hi, 
>>> >> There is an incompatibility between the latest Nvidia hardware and 
>>> the 
>>> >> TF that Warp ships with; see this thread 
>>> >> https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/Gryyk4cHp2M 
>>> >> 
>>> >> Best, 
>>> >> Pranav 
>>> >> -- 
>>> >> Pranav Shah 
>>> >> Postdoctoral Research Fellow. 
>>> >> 
>>> >> Division of Structural Biology, 
>>> >> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics, 
>>> >> University of Oxford, 
>>> >> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN, 
>>> >> UK 
>>> >> 
>>> >> On Wed, Jan 5, 2022 at 9:15 AM Olivier Le Bihan 
>>> >> <olivier....@outlook.com> wrote: 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > Hi there, 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > Let me rephrase my question; I would be interested to know if 
>>> anyone in here ever succeeded in making an RTX 3000 series GPU to work with 
>>> the BoxNet particle picking tool of Warp and what Nvidia driver / Cuda 
>>> version combinaison they used? 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > After a bit of googling, I found out many people have problems with 
>>> RTX3090 / Cuda 11 and TensorFlow 1.XX. Since Warp is using TensorFlow 1.1, 
>>> I thought maybe that is the root cause of my issues. 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > Could anyone please comment on that? 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > Best, 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > Olivier 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > On Thursday, 2 December 2021 at 14:15:30 UTC+1 Olivier Le Bihan 
>>> wrote: 
>>> >> >> 
>>> >> >> Hi all, 
>>> >> >> 
>>> >> >> I have the same problem with the exact same error message running 
>>> Warp 1.09 on a windows 10 threadripper machine with an RTX 3090 and 128 Gb 
>>> of RAM. 
>>> >> >> 
>>> >> >> Processing works fine otherwise except for the particle picking 
>>> tool. 
>>> >> >> 
>>> >> >> Would the Warp nightly build 2020-11-04 have any chance of fixing 
>>> the problem? 
>>> >> >> 
>>> >> >> Regards, 
>>> >> >> 
>>> >> >> Olivier Le Bihan 
>>> >> >> 
>>> >> >> On Wednesday, 1 December 2021 at 21:07:16 UTC+1 Dominique wrote: 
>>> >> >>> 
>>> >> >>> Hello I have a windows laptop with these specs 
>>> >> >>> 
>>> >> >>> Processor Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9750H CPU @ 2.60GHz 2.60 GHz 
>>> >> >>> Installed RAM 32.0 GB (31.8 GB usable) 
>>> >> >>> Device ID C7DFC50F-AE3A-4D98-8B8C-0790472B6BEF 
>>> >> >>> Product ID 00325-81709-59805-AAOEM 
>>> >> >>> System type 64-bit operating system, x64-based processor 
>>> >> >>> 1X Nvidia GeForce GTX 1660 Ti 
>>> >> >>> 
>>> >> >>> I installed WARP 1.09 on it and everything ran except for 
>>> particle picking. I get the error message in the screenshot. 
>>> >> > 
>>> >> > -- 
>>> >> > You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>>> Groups "Warp" group. 
>>> >> > To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, 
>>> send an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com. 
>>> >> > To view this discussion on the web visit 
>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/e4f9dc4b-deb5-46bc-95c2-675340a9e8b3n%40googlegroups.com. 
>>>
>>> > 
>>> > -- 
>>> > You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>>> Groups "Warp" group. 
>>> > To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send 
>>> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com. 
>>> > To view this discussion on the web visit 
>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/c99770f6-f34b-4fd2-b0a6-79880ef98a33n%40googlegroups.com. 
>>>
>>>
>>
